perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not, for itself or any other person or entity: (a) solicit, induce, recruit or encourage any of the Company's employees, contractors, independent contractors or any person who provides services to the Company to terminate or reduce their employment or other relationship with the Company; (b) hire any individual who is (or was within the six (6) months immediately preceding such hiring, unless such employee was terminated from such employment by Company) an employee, exclusive contractor, or exclusive independent contractor of the Company; or (c) attempt to do any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not, for itself or any other person or entity: (a) solicit, induce, recruit or encourage any of the Company's personnel, contractors, independent contractors or any person who provides services to the Company to terminate or reduce their employment or other relationship with the Company; (b) hire any individual who is (or was within the six (6) months immediately preceding such hiring, unless such employee was terminated from such employment by Company) an employee, exclusive contractor, or exclusive independent contractor of the Company; or (c) attempt to do any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'employees' is replaced with 'personnel' in Section 7.1(a). The contract does not define 'personnel' and it is unclear if it is meant to be synonymous with 'employees.' This creates a contradiction because the agreement restricts soliciting 'employees' and it could be argued that 'personnel' includes a broader category of workers, creating uncertainty. The contradiction is that, the agreement is not explicit on the differentiation between employees and personnel, therefore making the non-solicitation agreement itself unclear and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not, for itself or any other person or entity: (a) solicit, induce, recruit or encourage any of the Company's personnel, contractors, independent contractors or any person who provides services to the Company to terminate or reduce their employment or other relationship with the Company; (b) hire any individual who is (or was within the six (6) months immediately preceding such hiring, unless such employee was terminated from such employment by Company) an employee, exclusive contractor, or exclusive independent contractor of the Company; or (c) attempt to do any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'employees' to 'personnel' in Section 7.1(a) creates ambiguity because 'personnel' is not a defined term in the contract. The contradicted text uses both 'personnel' and 'employees,' raising uncertainty about the scope of the non-solicitation agreement. This uncertainty leads to a strong in-text contradiction, as it is unclear whether the restrictions apply only to 'employees' or to a broader category of 'personnel,' potentially making the agreement unenforceable due to the lack of clarity, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation and aligning with the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not: (a) solicit, entice or induce any Customer for the purpose of providing, or provide, products or services that are competitive with the products or services provided by the Company, or (b) solicit, entice, or induce any Customer to terminate or reduce its business with (or refrain from increasing its business with) the Company. As used in this Section 7.2, "Customer" means any person or entity to which the Company provided products or services, including any tenants of the Company's properties, (or was invested in real estate owned by the Company), and with which CONSULTANT had contact on behalf of the Company, within the last twelve (12) months of its consultancy with the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not: (a) solicit, entice or induce any Client for the purpose of providing, or provide, products or services that are competitive with the products or services provided by the Company, or (b) solicit, entice, or induce any Client to terminate or reduce its business with (or refrain from increasing its business with) the Company. As used in this Section 7.2, "Customer" means any person or entity to which the Company provided products or services, including any tenants of the Company's properties, (or was invested in real estate owned by the Company), and with which CONSULTANT had contact on behalf of the Company, within the last twelve (12) months of its consultancy with the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Customer' is replaced with 'Client' in the first part of section 7.2. However, the definition provided refers to 'Customer'. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the scope of the non-solicitation clause. It is unclear whether the restrictions apply to 'Clients' or only to 'Customers' as defined, or if those are interchangeable terms. The inconsistency in the terms, creates an internal contradiction in section 7.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not: (a) solicit, entice or induce any Client for the purpose of providing, or provide, products or services that are competitive with the products or services provided by the Company, or (b) solicit, entice, or induce any Client to terminate or reduce its business with (or refrain from increasing its business with) the Company. As used in this Section 7.2, "Customer" means any person or entity to which the Company provided products or services, including any tenants of the Company's properties, (or was invested in real estate owned by the Company), and with which CONSULTANT had contact on behalf of the Company, within the last twelve (12) months of its consultancy with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Customer" in relation to the non-solicitation clause. The changed text replaces "Customer" with "Client" in the operative clause, but the definition still refers to "Customer". This creates a clear contradiction as the term being used is not the term defined, leading to ambiguity about who is subject to the non-solicitation restriction, which directly creates a strong in-text contradiction within section 7.2 of the contract. The definition of 'Customer' does not apply to 'Client', thus creating uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 8.  Termination. This Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (a) upon the death or bankruptcy of CONSULTANT; or (b) upon the close of business on the date the Company gives CONSULTANT written notice of Termination for Cause (as defined below); (c) ninety (90) days following the date the Company gives CONSULTANT written notice of termination for any or no reason; or (d) CONSULTANT may terminate this Agreement by giving ninety (90) days' written notice to REIT. 4 For purposes of this Agreement, "Termination for Cause" shall mean termination of this Agreement by the Company as the result of: (i) any act of fraud, dishonesty or neglect of services by CONSULTANT in connection with the services to be provided under this Agreement or against any Company customer, vendor or affiliated company; or (ii) the breach or prospective breach of any provision of this Agreement by CONSULTANT.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.  Termination. This Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (a) upon the death or bankruptcy of CONSULTANT; or (b) upon the close of business on the date the Company gives CONSULTANT written notice of Termination for Breach (as defined below); (c) ninety (90) days following the date the Company gives CONSULTANT written notice of termination for any or no reason; or (d) CONSULTANT may terminate this Agreement by giving ninety (90) days' written notice to REIT. 4 For purposes of this Agreement, "Termination for Cause" shall mean termination of this Agreement by the Company as the result of: (i) any act of fraud, dishonesty or neglect of services by CONSULTANT in connection with the services to be provided under this Agreement or against any Company customer, vendor or affiliated company; or (ii) the breach or prospective breach of any provision of this Agreement by CONSULTANT.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 8(b), 'Termination for Cause' is changed to 'Termination for Breach.' However, the definition that follows still refers to 'Termination for Cause.' The document uses two terms interchangeably ('Termination for Cause' and 'Termination for Breach') without defining that those are synonymous. This creates a contradiction. It is unclear what the specific criteria for 'Termination for Breach' are, creating confusion regarding the circumstances under which the agreement can be terminated, since the definition provided in this section refers to 'Termination for Cause'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination. This Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (a) upon the death or bankruptcy of CONSULTANT; or (b) upon the close of business on the date the Company gives CONSULTANT written notice of Termination for Breach (as defined below); (c) ninety (90) days following the date the Company gives CONSULTANT written notice of termination for any or no reason; or (d) CONSULTANT may terminate this Agreement by giving ninety (90) days' written notice to REIT. 4 For purposes of this Agreement, "Termination for Cause" shall mean termination of this Agreement by the Company as the result of: (i) any act of fraud, dishonesty or neglect of services by CONSULTANT in connection with the services to be provided under this Agreement or against any Company customer, vendor or affiliated company; or (ii) the breach or prospective breach of any provision of this Agreement by CONSULTANT.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'Termination for Cause' with 'Termination for Breach' in Section 8(b). However, the subsequent definition still refers to 'Termination for Cause'. This creates a contradiction because the agreement now uses two different terms, 'Termination for Cause' and 'Termination for Breach', without clarifying if they are synonymous. This directly contradicts the intended meaning of the termination clause as it leaves ambiguity about what constitutes a 'Termination for Breach' and thus what triggers termination under Section 8(b), based on the definition given in the document.
